title: OpenCode Desktop Startup Freeze Incident
summary: OpenCode Desktop 1.16.0 starts its sidecar successfully but the renderer becomes unresponsive while loading the last active session in C:\development\02-Kx-to-process. Evidence points to persisted session/message data with very large diff payloads rather than a port, spawn, or raw SQLite integrity failure. The plan now includes upstream GitHub issue/release/commit research before local state or database mutation.
keyEvidence: main.log shows sidecar ready at http://127.0.0.1:53109
window.log shows renderer unresponsive in constructMessageRows after loadMessages
opencode.global.dat lastProject is C:\development\02-Kx-to-process and last session is ses_158d41ed8ffeZTg8Fa0jZwqAJG
The last session has a 25 MB user message containing summary diffs and a 2.3 MB patch part
Server logs also show repeated NOT NULL constraint failed: session_message.seq during scheduled run prompts on OpenCode 1.15.10 earlier on 2026-06-08
risks: Direct database edits could lose session history if performed without backup
The 1.16.2 updater is pending and may change behavior after install
Scheduled OpenCode jobs may continue to add bad or large session records while Desktop recovery is underway
Upstream issue search may reveal a safer workaround than local database archiving, so GitHub research must happen before heavier local changes
validationCriteria: Upstream GitHub findings are documented with issue/release/commit URLs or an explicit no-match result
OpenCode Desktop opens without renderer unresponsive events for at least 60 seconds
A safe project can be opened and a new session can be created
The problematic 02-Kx session is either archived/avoided or confirmed fixed
Hourly scheduled OpenCode jobs no longer emit session_message.seq errors
Any state/database mutation has a timestamped backup and rollback command
